page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 2864.18 and high text processing capabilities in Geekbench. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 5734.87 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4217.0. Memory subsystem performance is a notable bottleneck; the instance registers poor memory scores in Passmark and weak uncached memory read and write bandwidth. Consequently, operations that rely heavily on memory throughput, such as Redis SET operations and static web serving, show lower performance metrics.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point into dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, though its value is constrained by the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and limited memory bandwidth. It is best suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight text processing, basic cryptographic operations, and small-scale utility workloads. It is not recommended for memory-intensive databases, large-scale software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference due to severe performance degradation on larger models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance exhibits strong single-core CPU performance and solid text processing capabilities, but is constrained by weak multi-core scaling and poor memory bandwidth. It represents a cost-effective option for dedicated compute workloads that do not require high memory density. This instance is best suited for single-threaded applications, basic cryptography, and lightweight text processing, while being poorly suited for memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight, CPU-bound workloads with minimal memory requirements.

Strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptographic acceleration
